specific_concerns: The 'stranger-test' is a very contemporary heuristic; some practitioners might view it as reducing a doctrinally weighty verse to a clever decision-rule.
The 'principal who still occasionally references at all-hands' framing is colloquial — but appropriate to the engineering domain.
verdict_reason: The verse is honored. The Sanskrit objection is quoted. The doctrinal reading (misplaced loyalty as paralysis-mechanism, sincere but framed wrongly) is preserved. The verse explicitly preserves the case where the loyalty is right (refusal-as-inquiry, present-architect collaboration) — this prevents the verse from being used as a hammer. A serious practitioner would find the verse's diagnostic move preserved. The Krishna/Arjuna dialogic position is preserved by the framing 'the verse names the trap; the chapter has not yet dissolved it.' Not flippant.
